seed: softness vs severity; signal_vs_noise; open_gate; spiral_counterclockwise; pulse_marks against radial; bottom_weighted; light_vs_dark; tiny_plus; guilt_forgiveness | abstract variation request 514; target batch count 500
title: Contrast and Harmony
reasoning: This artwork embodies the emotional tension between softness and severity, reflected in a divided composition. The left half features a patchwork of gentle pinks and whites, creating a flow of warmth and subtlety. This softness is disrupted by the right side's harsh black and crimson accents, symbolizing severity and cold boundaries. The central collision is marked by a blend of pattern types; the smooth patchwork interrupts the rigid cracks of severity, creating an emotional echo. The palette emphasizes the contrast through light versus dark, highlighting various emotional states that intertwine through the design. The presence of broken pixels on the severity side introduces intentional imperfection, maintaining the complexity of feelings like guilt and relief. This visual narrative moves from warmth to a stark pressure, celebrating the balance of opposing emotions.

layout.name: split_vertical
layout.description: Left 4 columns are emotion A, right 4 columns are emotion B.

composition.name: order_vs_chaos
composition.idea: One side follows a clean pattern while the other breaks apart.

referenceExample.title: Desire Held Back
referenceExample.pattern: warm saturated pull stopped by a cool sealed boundary

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
